title: The End Times Are A Geopolitical Machine
subtitle: A cleaned reading of the Sneako interview with Jiang Xueqin and Aleksandr Dugin
voice_policy: Direct argumentative prose derived from the interview. Preserve the pressure of the exchange and avoid repeated third-person framing. Do not impersonate first-person authorship except when quoting verified source material.
trace_policy: Paragraph-level transcript refs. Speculative historical chains are attributed as Jiang or Dugin claims, not promoted as established canon.
dek: This is not a neutral theology panel. Sneako brings Jiang and Dugin together because eschatology has become a way to read geopolitics: Russia, Persia, Zionism, American Protestantism, China, finance, Epstein, Trump, and the Middle East all become signs in a fight over the end of history. Jiang supplies the machinery: the Bank of England turns capital into empire, Calvinism turns wealth into chosenness and fear, Christian Zionism makes Zionism into an instrument, and Greater North America becomes America's possible last fortress against Eurasia.
thesis: The interview turns end-times belief into a map of power. Jiang defines eschatology as the question of where history ends and where God and man reunify. From there, his argument is material as much as spiritual: Western values come from finance, property, skepticism, utilitarian pleasure, and Calvinist anxiety; America is rich but spiritually sick; Christian Zionism should be read as a system that weaponizes fear and prophecy; a Russia-China-Iran Eurasian bloc threatens the dollar order; and Greater North America is the possible attempt to replace a failing financial empire with a resource empire. Dugin gives the Orthodox answer: resist Antichrist, never side with evil, and keep the catechon even if the end is coming. Jiang's final difference is colder: the anti-human system is not only evil. It is attractive. People may fight to keep it because it brought prosperity, technology, and comfort.

opening.heading: Core Reading
opening.text: The sharp move is that eschatology stops being a private belief about heaven and becomes a political technology. Jiang defines it as each tradition's understanding of the endpoint of history: why we are here, where we are going, and the point where God and man reunify. That definition lets every later question become geopolitical. Zionism, Orthodox Russia, Islam, China, American Protestantism, Trump, Epstein, BRICS, the dollar, and Iran are not separate topics. They are competing answers to what history is for.

marks.text: the endpoint of history
God and man reunify
a political technology
competing answers to what history is for

beats.heading: Why Jiang Is In The Room
beats.summary: The setup makes Jiang unusual: a Chinese atheist-turned-Gnostic reader of Kabbalah, geopolitics, and end-times traditions.

paragraphs.text: Dugin's first point is that Chinese culture is usually balanced, cyclical, and harmony-seeking rather than organized around a final battle. That is why Jiang matters in this conversation. Dugin calls him exceptional because Jiang can move through Zionist, Islamic, Russian Orthodox, and Kabbalistic end-times frameworks from inside a Chinese civilizational background that does not naturally center the radical feeling of the end.

marks.text: radical feeling of the end
exceptional because Jiang can move through Zionist, Islamic, Russian Orthodox, and Kabbalistic end-times frameworks

paragraphs.text: Jiang's own self-placement is not church loyalty. He says he grew up in atheist China, spent most of his life skeptical of religion, and only in the last two years moved toward a Gnostic framework through eschatology, Kabbalah, and geopolitics. That matters because he is not answering as a priest. He is reading belief as world-model, empire logic, and historical force.

marks.text: not church loyalty
belief as world-model, empire logic, and historical force

beats.heading: Finance Invents The West
beats.summary: Jiang does not start Western decay with culture-war images. He starts in 1694, with the Bank of England and infinite financing.

paragraphs.text: When the host asks about decaying Western values, Jiang does not begin with TV shows, decadence, or Trump's Easter posts. He begins with 1694: the Bank of England. The bank lets transnational capital lend to the nation-state with Parliament as guarantor. Kings used to run out of money, die, or refuse repayment. The new machine lets Britain keep borrowing and keep fighting until Napoleon is finally exhausted. Empire is born as a financial technology.

marks.text: 1694: the Bank of England
Empire is born as a financial technology

paragraphs.text: The philosophy follows the machinery. Locke sanctifies private property. Hume weakens confidence in truth and intuition. Bentham makes pleasure the measure of good. Dugin then pushes the chain through social Darwinism, Ayn Rand, Calvinist wealth theology, and a Protestant America cut off from older sacred roots. The result is a civilization where wealth can look like divine election, poverty can look like damnation, and people become hostages of their own economic theology.

marks.text: people become hostages of their own economic theology
wealth can look like divine election

beats.heading: Christian Zionism As Fear Machine
beats.summary: The Scofield Bible, Calvinist anxiety, and Gog-and-Magog interpretation turn theology into geopolitics.

paragraphs.text: The Scofield Bible becomes the episode's example of commentary turning into scripture. Dugin says a small Protestant sect embedded its end-times interpretation inside the Bible, making many readers unable to distinguish holy text from pseudo-prophecy. In that frame, Russia becomes Gog, Islam becomes Amalek, and fighting Russians and Muslims becomes religious duty.

marks.text: commentary turning into scripture
fighting Russians and Muslims becomes religious duty

paragraphs.text: Jiang tightens the chronology: Herzl, Scofield, Balfour, all close together in his telling. Then he brings the argument back to American emotion. Calvinism is still alive as the gospel of wealth, and its underlying emotions are fear and anxiety. America can be the richest society in human history, with an average middle-class life better than a Roman emperor, and still become depressed, medicated, and polarized. If America is to redeem itself, it has to re-examine the Christian principles underneath the machine.

marks.text: fear and anxiety
richer than Rome and still depressed

paragraphs.text: The most provocative reversal comes when the host asks whether evangelical Christianity is a tool of Zionism. Jiang says the inverse may be closer: Zionism can be read as a tool of Christian Zionists. The claim is speculative and should stay attributed, but its function in the interview is clear. It makes political Zionism part of a Protestant eschatological machine rather than simply an outside lobby acting on America.

marks.text: Zionism can be read as a tool of Christian Zionists

paragraphs.text: Dugin answers from the Russian side. Russian Orthodoxy respects Judaism as a separate faith, rejects Christian Zionism, and reads Putin pragmatically. Orthodox Russians may project a catechonic role onto Putin, but Putin himself remains a political realist. The present danger, for Dugin, is that Trump's Christian Zionism and aggressive Israeli Zionism now look like confirmation of older Orthodox fears.

marks.text: Orthodox Russians may project a catechonic role onto Putin
confirmation of older Orthodox fears

beats.heading: Eurasia Or Greater North America
beats.summary: Jiang turns the dollar, BRICS, gold, Iran, oil, and Greenland into one strategic fork.

paragraphs.text: Jiang's alternative to American hegemony is Eurasian unity: Russia, China, and Iran using BRICS as a frame, extending toward Europe, Africa, and Southeast Asia. The financial core is a gold corridor, a distributed gold-backed system that could become the basis of trade beyond the dollar. That is why the idea is existential for America. If people stop buying Treasuries, the $39 trillion debt cannot be financed. The empire collapses not first as battlefield defeat, but as a confidence failure.

marks.text: gold corridor
the empire collapses first as confidence failure

paragraphs.text: That is where Greater North America enters. Jiang imagines a continental fortress: Greenland, Canada, Mexico, Venezuela, Colombia, Cuba, Nicaragua, Honduras, oil, minerals, food, shipping routes, and North American safety behind two oceans. The plan would shift America from financial empire to resource empire. Destroy or destabilize the GCC and the Middle East, and Europe and East Asia are forced to buy energy, financing, and weapons through North America.

marks.text: continental fortress
from financial empire to resource empire

paragraphs.text: Dugin accepts the possibility that Trump is not only madness. He may also be a weapon used by a deeper state to preserve Western hegemony through naked power, oil access, and global war, then sacrificed when the work is done. Against that, Dugin offers multipolarity: Russia does not want to occupy Europe or Asia; it wants a world where Russian, Chinese, Indian, Islamic, and Western poles exist without Western supremacy.

marks.text: Trump as weapon, then scapegoat
multipolarity

beats.heading: Scapegoats And The End-Times Map
beats.summary: Jiang refuses a single puppet-master theory and turns the deep state into a convergence of interests.

paragraphs.text: The host asks if the deep state is simply Chabad Lubavitch. Jiang's answer is more useful than a single-name theory: convergence of interests. Military contractors, security agencies, secret societies, Wall Street, the City of London, Trump, Kushner, Witkoff, and Chabad do not need to be one command center. Different factions may all need the Iran war. The visible people become vanguard and scapegoat while stronger actors work behind the scenes.

marks.text: convergence of interests
vanguard and scapegoat

paragraphs.text: Dugin then separates Russia's treatment of religious communities from American anxieties. Lubavitch exists in Russia as one Jewish religious current. Islam is indigenous to Russia, not merely immigrant. Jews, Muslims, Buddhists, and other traditional religions are accepted as communities, while Putin is described as a practicing Orthodox Christian motivated by Russian interests.

marks.text: Islam is indigenous to Russia
Putin is described as a practicing Orthodox Christian motivated by Russian interests

paragraphs.text: The Middle East is where the two maps overlap. Dugin says Russia can read events through realist geopolitics and through the geography of prophecy at the same time. That makes Jiang's question sharper: if catechon is the force restraining Antichrist, why restrain it at all? Why not let Antichrist consume the world and get to salvation faster?

marks.text: realist geopolitics and the geography of prophecy at the same time
why restrain it at all?

beats.heading: Never Side With Evil
beats.summary: Dugin answers Jiang with catechonic ethics, then Jiang agrees with the anti-human diagnosis while preparing his objection.

paragraphs.text: Dugin's answer is moral before it is strategic. The catechon delays Antichrist. A negative catechon might say: let evil rule briefly so Christ returns sooner. Dugin rejects that path. Never side with evil, even if evil has a place in God's plan. Stay against Antichrist until the last breath. That is the Orthodox state mission as he understands it.

marks.text: Never side with evil
stay against Antichrist until the last breath

paragraphs.text: Epstein then becomes more than scandal. For Dugin, the files confirm what Orthodox elders said about the West: the veil is lifted, and modern Western civilization appears as Antichrist. But he refuses determinism. There is no predestination. The human soul is free. Americans can welcome Antichrist, or reject that role and become human again.

marks.text: Epstein becomes more than scandal
Americans can welcome Antichrist, or reject that role

paragraphs.text: Jiang agrees overall that Western civilization is built on anti-human ideas: individuality, rationality, science, consumerism, utilitarianism. But his agreement is not the end of the argument. His difference is that these ideas are not merely repulsive. They are attractive, materially productive, and psychologically seductive.

marks.text: anti-human ideas
attractive, materially productive, and psychologically seductive

beats.heading: The System Is Attractive
beats.summary: Jiang's final pressure is social realism: many people will fight for comfort, prosperity, and information overload rather than resist Antichrist.

paragraphs.text: This is Jiang's deepest disagreement with Dugin. Russia may feel urgency to fight Antichrist, but materialist societies such as China ask how to keep the system because it has brought happiness and prosperity. The West gives people the internet, wealth creation, space travel, comfort, and spectacle. The question is not only what is evil. The question is how many people will choose to resist when the evil works well enough to be loved.

marks.text: how many people will choose to resist
when the evil works well enough to be loved

paragraphs.text: The host adds the media form of the same problem: not old censorship, but flooding. Gaza, Lebanon, Epstein, jobs, debt, personal exhaustion, memes, and algorithmic noise all arrive at once until people have no energy left to care. Jiang's practical answer is not grandiose. Have the conversation. Refuse the name-calling frame. A transnational conversation is already resistance to the managed script.

marks.text: not old censorship, but flooding
A transnational conversation is already resistance to the managed script

paragraphs.text: Dugin closes by accepting that materialism tempts Russia and China too. Only a small minority consciously understands the catechonic mission. China's futuristic cities may be miraculous and poisonous at once: proud, prosperous, technologically perfect, and empty of children. The final moral returns to freedom. Americans, Chinese, Russians, Muslims: no one can be forced into the decision. The demon enters only when invited.

marks.text: futuristic cities may be miraculous and poisonous at once
The demon enters only when invited

questions.question: What is eschatology?
questions.answer: Jiang says eschatology is each religious tradition's understanding of the endpoint of history: why we are here, where we are going, where we come from, and the point where God and man reunify.

questions.question: Would you classify your belief system as general Gnosticism, not specific to one theology?
questions.answer: Jiang says he grew up atheist in China and was skeptical of religion, especially Christianity, but recent study of eschatology, Kabbalah, and geopolitics has moved him toward a Gnostic framework without one religious loyalty.

questions.question: Is Christian Zionism, or evangelical Christianity more broadly, just a tool of Zionism?
questions.answer: Jiang reverses the direction: he says one can argue Zionism is a tool created by Christian Zionists to fulfill their eschatology, through a speculative chain involving Frankists, Freemasons, Christian Zionism, America, and political Zionism.

questions.question: Are Chinese people aware of Professor Dugin's theory of Eurasian unity, and could Russia and China unite around it?
questions.answer: Jiang says a Russia-China-Iran bloc inside BRICS, possibly using a gold-based financial corridor, could become an alternative to American hegemony. The problem is that such a system would threaten U.S. Treasuries and could bankrupt America.

questions.question: Is the deep state simply Chabad Lubavitch?
questions.answer: Jiang says it is better to think in terms of convergence of interests. Chabad Lubavitch, Kushner, Witkoff, Trump, and other visible actors may be vanguards or scapegoats, while military, security, secret-society, Wall Street, and City of London interests also benefit from war with Iran.

source_note: This is a three-person Sneako interview published April 13, 2026 with Jiang Xueqin and Aleksandr Dugin. The transcript has noisy speaker diarization and several ASR errors around names such as Scofield and catechon, so the read attributes claims by conversational role and named address. Speculative chains about Frankists, Freemasons, Chabad, Epstein, and deep-state actors are presented as source claims, not as verified historical findings.
